name: rg
description: Ripgrep is a line-oriented search tool that recursively searches the current directory for a regex pattern. It respects .gitignore rules and automatically skips hidden files/directories and binary files.

globalOptions.name: --regexp
globalOptions.description: A pattern to search for. This option can be provided multiple times, where all patterns given are searched

globalOptions.name: --fixed-strings
globalOptions.description: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression

globalOptions.name: --ignore-case
globalOptions.description: Search case insensitively. This overrides --case-sensitive and --smart-case

globalOptions.name: --smart-case
globalOptions.description: Search case insensitively if the pattern is all lowercase. Otherwise, search case sensitively

globalOptions.name: --case-sensitive
globalOptions.description: Search case sensitively. This overrides --ignore-case and --smart-case

globalOptions.name: --word-regexp
globalOptions.description: Only show matches surrounded by word boundaries. This is equivalent to putting \b before and after the search pattern

globalOptions.name: --line-regexp
globalOptions.description: Only show matches surrounded by line boundaries. This is equivalent to putting ^...$ around the search pattern

globalOptions.name: --invert-match
globalOptions.description: Invert matching. Show lines that do not match the given patterns

globalOptions.name: --count
globalOptions.description: Only show the count of matching lines for each file. This suppresses line content output

globalOptions.name: --count-matches
globalOptions.description: Only show the count of individual matches for each file. This differs from --count in that it counts every match, not just lines

globalOptions.name: --files-with-matches
globalOptions.description: Only print the paths of files that contain at least one match

globalOptions.name: --files-without-match
globalOptions.description: Only print the paths of files that contain zero matches

globalOptions.name: --max-count
globalOptions.description: Limit the number of matching lines per file searched

globalOptions.name: --json
globalOptions.description: Print results in JSON Lines format. Each line is a JSON object with a 'type' field indicating the kind of result

globalOptions.name: --no-line-number
globalOptions.description: Suppress line numbers. This is enabled by default when not searching in a terminal

globalOptions.name: --line-number
globalOptions.description: Show line numbers (1-based). This is enabled by default when searching in a terminal

globalOptions.name: --column
globalOptions.description: Show column numbers (1-based). This only shows the column number of the first match on each line

globalOptions.name: --only-matching
globalOptions.description: Print only the matched (non-empty) parts of a matching line, with each such part on a separate output line

globalOptions.name: --replace
globalOptions.description: Replace every match with the text given. Capture group indices ($1, $2, etc.) and names ($name) are supported

globalOptions.name: --passthru
globalOptions.description: Print both matching and non-matching lines. Non-matching lines do not trigger any replacements when --replace is used

globalOptions.name: --no-unicode
globalOptions.description: Disable unicode-aware search. By default, ripgrep will use unicode-aware matching

globalOptions.name: --glob
globalOptions.description: Include or exclude files and directories matching the given glob. Prefix with '!' to exclude. Can be specified multiple times

globalOptions.name: --iglob
globalOptions.description: Like --glob, but the pattern is matched case insensitively

globalOptions.name: --type
globalOptions.description: Only search files matching the given type. Use --type-list to see available types. Can be specified multiple times

globalOptions.name: --type-not
globalOptions.description: Do not search files matching the given type. Inverse of --type

globalOptions.name: --type-add
globalOptions.description: Add a new file type. Format: NAME:GLOB (e.g. 'web:*.{html,css,js}')

globalOptions.name: --type-list
globalOptions.description: Show all supported file types and their corresponding globs

globalOptions.name: --max-depth
globalOptions.description: Limit the depth of directory traversal to the given number of levels. A value of 0 searches only the start path

globalOptions.name: --follow
globalOptions.description: Follow symbolic links while traversing directories

globalOptions.name: --hidden
globalOptions.description: Search hidden files and directories. By default, hidden files and directories are skipped

globalOptions.name: --no-ignore
globalOptions.description: Do not respect ignore files (.gitignore, .ignore, etc.)

globalOptions.name: --no-ignore-vcs
globalOptions.description: Do not respect version control ignore files (.gitignore, etc.) but still respect .ignore files

globalOptions.name: --no-ignore-global
globalOptions.description: Do not respect the global gitignore file

globalOptions.name: --ignore-file
globalOptions.description: Specify an additional ignore file to use for filtering

globalOptions.name: --binary
globalOptions.description: Search binary files. By default, binary files are skipped entirely

globalOptions.name: --compressed
globalOptions.description: Search in compressed files (gzip, bzip2, xz, LZ4, LZMA, Brotli, Zstd)

globalOptions.name: --stats
globalOptions.description: Print aggregate statistics about the search results at the end

globalOptions.name: --trim
globalOptions.description: Trim prefixed whitespace from all output lines. Useful when indentation is not relevant

globalOptions.name: --context
globalOptions.description: Show NUM lines before and after each match. This overrides --before-context and --after-context

globalOptions.name: --before-context
globalOptions.description: Show NUM lines before each match

globalOptions.name: --after-context
globalOptions.description: Show NUM lines after each match

globalOptions.name: --color
globalOptions.description: Controls when to use color. Values: never, auto, always, ansi

globalOptions.name: --colors
globalOptions.description: Configure color settings for specific parts of output. Format: TYPE:ATTRIBUTE:VALUE (e.g. 'match:fg:red')

globalOptions.name: --sort
globalOptions.description: Sort results in ascending order by the given criteria: none, path, modified, accessed, created

globalOptions.name: --sortr
globalOptions.description: Sort results in descending order by the given criteria: none, path, modified, accessed, created

globalOptions.name: --threads
globalOptions.description: The approximate number of threads to use for searching. A value of 0 (default) causes ripgrep to choose automatically

globalOptions.name: --multiline
globalOptions.description: Enable matching across multiple lines. When enabled, patterns may match across line boundaries

globalOptions.name: --multiline-dotall
globalOptions.description: When used with --multiline, '.' will also match newline characters

globalOptions.name: --pcre2
globalOptions.description: Use the PCRE2 regex engine instead of the default. This enables lookaround and backreferences

globalOptions.name: --engine
globalOptions.description: Specify which regular expression engine to use. Values: default, pcre2, auto

globalOptions.name: --pre
globalOptions.description: Run a preprocessor on every searched file and search the output of the preprocessor instead

globalOptions.name: --pre-glob
globalOptions.description: Limit the preprocessor to files matching the given glob. Only applies when --pre is used

globalOptions.name: --search-zip
globalOptions.description: Search in compressed files. Requires that lz4, bzip2, gzip or xz is in your PATH

globalOptions.name: --one-file-system
globalOptions.description: Do not cross filesystem boundaries when traversing directories

globalOptions.name: --unrestricted
globalOptions.description: Reduce the level of smart filtering. -u includes hidden files, -uu includes hidden files and ignores .gitignore, -uuu searches binary files too

detectors.name: rg_type_list
detectors.description: List available file types for filtering
